name: @vanillagreen/pi-qol
description: Pi quality-of-life extension: compact statusline/π prompt, reliable multiline input, styled pasted-image chips, session naming/search/context import, scheduled prompts, handoff, permission prompts, notifications, custom compaction, and a collapsed-thinking timer.

settings.label: Enable QOL editor helpers
settings.description: Install the QOL editor component and /qol helper command. Disable and reload to leave Pi editor behavior unchanged.

settings.label: Glyph style
settings.description: Glyphs for vstack-owned UI chrome. Unicode is the default; ASCII uses plain box/status/separator characters for terminal compatibility. pi-tool-renderer's global override wins when set.

settings.label: Show compact statusline
settings.description: Render the QOL statusline row with repo, branch, model, thinking level, and context percentage. Disable when another statusline/footer extension owns this UI.

settings.label: Replace built-in footer
settings.description: Hide Pi default footer while the compact QOL statusline is enabled.

settings.label: Use π prompt editor
settings.description: Replace the main editor with the compact π prompt editor. Disable to use QOL newline/image helpers with Pi default editor chrome.

settings.label: Show session name title
settings.description: Show the named session above the prompt. In tmux, update the current pane title to "π <session>" and enable a pane border title padded with non-breaking spaces so tmux does not trim it.

settings.label: Sync session name to tmux window name
settings.description: Also rename the current tmux window to "π <session>" so the session name shows in the tmux status line. Disables tmux automatic-rename for this window while active and restores the original window name and automatic-rename setting on shutdown.

settings.label: Input bottom padding
settings.description: Blank lines below the compact prompt. Default is 0 for no extra space.

settings.label: Git refresh timeout
settings.description: Timeout for each git metadata command used by the statusline.

settings.label: Show dirty marker
settings.description: Append * to the branch badge when the worktree is dirty.

settings.label: Prompt before risky bash commands
settings.description: Ask for confirmation before bash tool calls matching the configured command list. Off by default; enable explicitly if you want this extra prompt.

settings.label: Commands to prompt for
settings.description: Comma-separated command fragments to confirm before running. Literal entries are case-insensitive and whitespace-tolerant; regex entries may use /pattern/flags. Default prompts only for rm -Rf.

settings.label: Approval preview lines
settings.description: Maximum command lines rendered in the permission approval prompt. Longer commands show a head/tail preview with an omitted-line marker.

settings.label: Approval preview characters
settings.description: Maximum command preview characters rendered in the permission approval prompt to avoid flooding or flickering the terminal.

settings.label: shift+enter inserts newline
settings.description: Intercept distinguishable shift+enter/shift+return in the prompt editor and insert a newline instead of submitting.

settings.label: Fallback newline key
settings.description: Alternate newline key for terminals that cannot distinguish shift+enter. alt+enter is reserved for Pi follow-up messages.

settings.label: Style pending queue preview
settings.description: Render Pi's built-in steering/follow-up queue preview in ANSI green with a heavy left queue bar, while keeping the alt+up restore hint in the theme hint color with a two-space indent.

settings.label: Style image chips
settings.description: Render [Image #N] placeholders and existing pasted image paths in the editor as visually distinct chips. Pi still owns attachment state.

settings.label: Show attachment count
settings.description: Show a lightweight status badge when the draft contains image placeholders.

settings.label: Enable /rename command
settings.description: Register the /rename command for setting or viewing the current session's friendly name in Pi's session selector.

settings.label: Auto-name new sessions
settings.description: Automatically generate a friendly session name from the first user prompt when the session does not already have a name.

settings.label: Auto-rename model
settings.description: Model used for title generation. Use provider/model, current, or cheapest. Default is a fast OpenAI Codex model that supports ChatGPT OAuth via /login openai-codex.

settings.label: Auto-rename fallback model
settings.description: Model to try if the primary model is unavailable. Use current, provider/model, cheapest, none, or off.

settings.label: Deterministic fallback
settings.description: Fallback naming strategy if all model calls fail: title-case words from the first prompt, a truncated prompt, or none.

settings.label: Auto-rename prefix
settings.description: Optional static prefix added before generated names, e.g. a project label. Leave empty for no prefix.

settings.label: Auto-rename input cap
settings.description: Maximum first-prompt characters sent to the naming model. Long prompts keep the beginning and end.

settings.label: Auto-rename title length
settings.description: Maximum characters for generated session names, including any prefix.

settings.label: Auto-rename output tokens
settings.description: Maximum output tokens requested from the naming model.

settings.label: Auto-rename timeout
settings.description: Per-model timeout in milliseconds before falling back to the next model or deterministic title.

settings.label: Auto-rename prompt override
settings.description: Optional prompt template. Use {{message}} for the first user prompt; when empty, QOL uses its built-in concise title prompt.

settings.label: Notify on auto-rename
settings.description: Show a Pi UI notification when QOL auto-generates a session name. Manual /qol rename always notifies.

settings.label: Debug auto-rename
settings.description: Show model/auth/fallback diagnostics for automatic session naming.

settings.label: Enable /handoff command
settings.description: Register the handoff command to generate a focused prompt and create a new session with the prompt as a draft.

settings.label: Enable /schedule command
settings.description: Register /schedule for timer-based user messages, e.g. /schedule 20m retry after rate reset. The timer runs in the extension without invoking the model immediately.

settings.label: Auto-resume after rate limits
settings.description: When Pi detects a rate-limit stop with a reset time, schedule a follow-up user message at the reset time so the agent can continue if nothing else has continued since.

settings.label: Auto-resume message
settings.description: User message sent after a detected rate limit resets.
settings.default: Hit API rate limit. It has been reset. Continue

settings.label: Reset buffer seconds
settings.description: Seconds to wait after the reported reset time before sending the auto-resume message.

settings.label: Maximum auto-resume delay
settings.description: Ignore detected reset times farther away than this many minutes, to avoid stale or malformed schedules.

settings.label: Notify on auto-resume
settings.description: Show Pi UI notices when rate-limit auto-resume is scheduled, sent, or fails.

settings.label: Review handoff prompt
settings.description: Open an editor to review or edit the generated handoff prompt before creating the new session.

settings.label: Enable /context command
settings.description: Register the /context command to show inline context-window usage with estimated Pi/model category breakdowns.

settings.label: Enable session search
settings.description: Register the previous-session search palette, preview, resume, and context-import actions.

settings.label: Session search shortcut
settings.description: Keyboard shortcut to open the session search overlay. Default f2 avoids Pi's built-in ctrl+f and alt+f editor bindings. Set to none to disable.

settings.label: Session search result limit
settings.description: Maximum number of matching prompts returned by the search palette.

settings.label: Visible session rows
settings.description: Maximum number of sessions shown at once in the overlay before scrolling.

settings.label: Preview snippets
settings.description: Maximum matching snippets shown on the selected session preview screen.

settings.label: Overlay width
settings.description: Preferred session search overlay width in terminal columns.

settings.label: Visible user prompt rows
settings.description: Maximum number of one-line user prompts shown at once in the session prompt browser before scrolling.

settings.label: Session cache TTL
settings.description: Seconds before the in-memory session list is refreshed. Default 0 keeps the cache until you explicitly run /search refresh.

settings.label: Session summary model
settings.description: Model used to summarize an imported previous session. Use current, provider/model, or a bare model id. Defaults to current so no extra provider setup is required.

settings.label: Session summary max output tokens
settings.description: Maximum tokens requested from the session-search summarizer.

settings.label: Summary input character cap
settings.description: Maximum previous-session transcript characters sent to the summarizer; long sessions keep the beginning and end.

settings.label: Enable notifications
settings.description: Send terminal/tmux notifications for ready/input-needed, questions, completed task lists, and critical information.

settings.label: Notify when ready
settings.description: Notify when an agent turn finishes and Pi is ready for input.

settings.label: Notify when direction needed
settings.description: Use stronger notification wording when the last assistant message appears to ask for direction, confirmation, or a choice.

settings.label: Notify for question popups
settings.description: Notify when pi-questions opens a structured question that requires user input.

settings.label: Notify when all tasks complete
settings.description: Notify after the agent turn ends when tasks_write completes the task panel.

settings.label: Notify critical info
settings.description: Notify for assistant messages containing critical, blocked, security, rate-limit, or cannot-proceed signals. Tool failures are intentionally ignored.

settings.label: Terminal bell
settings.description: Emit BEL (\u0007) for standard terminal/tmux audible or visual bell behavior.

settings.label: Mute bell sound
settings.description: Keep notifications enabled while suppressing QOL-emitted BEL bytes; OSC terminal notifications use ST terminators where supported.

settings.label: Bell when tmux window active
settings.description: Also emit BEL when the source tmux window is already active. Disabled by default to avoid noisy bells while you are looking at the Pi window; native terminal notifications still run so Ghostty can decide based on app focus.

settings.label: Native terminal notification
settings.description: Send OSC 777/OSC 99 terminal notifications, or Windows toast in Windows Terminal/WSL.

settings.label: tmux display-message
settings.description: When running inside tmux, also show a transient tmux status-line message. Disabled by default because tmux displays it on the active client, not necessarily as a source-window mark.

settings.label: Mark tmux window
settings.description: Optionally prefix the source tmux window name when notifying. Disabled by default because BEL should let tmux apply your normal bell styling.

settings.label: tmux window mark text
settings.description: Prefix used when marking the source tmux window.

settings.label: tmux window mark duration
settings.description: Milliseconds before automatically clearing the tmux window mark. 0 keeps it until next input/agent start/reset.

settings.label: tmux native via client TTY
settings.description: When inside tmux, send OSC terminal notifications directly to attached tmux client TTYs so Ghostty/system notifications still appear for inactive source windows.

settings.label: tmux OSC passthrough
settings.description: Wrap OSC terminal notifications in tmux passthrough sequences so supported terminals outside tmux can receive them.

settings.label: Also show Pi UI notice
settings.description: Also call ctx.ui.notify for QOL external notifications. Disabled by default to avoid duplicate in-session noise.

settings.label: Terminal notification protocol
settings.description: OSC notification protocol. auto uses Kitty OSC 99 when KITTY_WINDOW_ID is present, otherwise OSC 777.

settings.label: Notification cooldown
settings.description: Minimum seconds between duplicate notifications of the same kind.

settings.label: Notification title
settings.description: Title used for external terminal notifications.

settings.label: Ready message
settings.description: Body text for normal agent-done notifications.

settings.label: Notification body length
settings.description: Maximum body characters for external notifications.

settings.label: tmux message duration
settings.description: Milliseconds to display tmux status-line notification messages.

settings.label: Custom compaction summaries
settings.description: Use QOL's custom compaction hook instead of Pi's default summary generation. Manual/auto compaction still uses Pi's normal trigger settings.

settings.label: Compaction model
settings.description: Summarizer model as provider/model, a bare model id, or current. Default uses Pi's active model.

settings.label: Compaction detail profile
settings.description: Summary detail level for custom compaction and branch summaries.

settings.label: Compaction max output tokens
settings.description: Maximum tokens requested from the custom compaction summarizer.

settings.label: Include previous summary
settings.description: Pass the previous compaction summary to the custom summarizer for iterative continuity.

settings.label: Fallback to Pi default compaction
settings.description: If custom compaction fails, let Pi run its default compaction instead of cancelling the compaction.

settings.label: Show compaction notifications
settings.description: Show QOL notifications when custom, remote, branch, or idle compaction starts/fails/completes.

settings.label: Remote compaction endpoint
settings.description: Use a remote HTTP summarization endpoint before falling back to the configured model. Endpoint receives { systemPrompt, prompt, maxTokens } and returns { summary }.

settings.label: Remote endpoint URL
settings.description: HTTP endpoint for remote compaction summaries. Leave empty to use the configured compaction model.

settings.label: Custom branch summaries
settings.description: When /tree navigation requests a branch summary, generate it through QOL's custom summarizer.

settings.label: Idle compaction trigger
settings.description: Trigger Pi compaction after the session sits idle above the configured token threshold. Inspired by oh-my-pi idle compaction.

settings.label: Idle token threshold
settings.description: Token count above which QOL idle compaction can trigger when thresholdTokens and thresholdPercent are unset.

settings.label: Idle compaction delay
settings.description: Seconds to wait idle before triggering QOL idle compaction.

settings.label: QOL compaction token limit
settings.description: Optional fixed token limit for QOL idle compaction. Set -1 to use percentage or idle token threshold.

settings.label: QOL compaction percent limit
settings.description: Optional context-window percentage for QOL idle compaction. Set -1 to use idle token threshold.

settings.label: Long-session budget guard
settings.description: Trigger compaction on agent_end (no idle wait) when context usage crosses budgetPercent or budgetTokens. Designed for long autonomous runs so transcript growth cannot silently hit provider/buffer limits. Fires once per threshold crossing, not in a loop.

settings.label: Budget guard percent
settings.description: Context-window percentage that fires the budget guard at agent_end. Set -1 to disable percent-based firing and rely on absolute tokens instead.

settings.label: Budget guard token limit
settings.description: Absolute token count above which the budget guard fires at agent_end. Set -1 to use only budgetPercent.

settings.label: Chunked compaction input cap
settings.description: Maximum serialized characters sent to the summarizer in a single request. Inputs larger than this are split into chunks, summarized chunk-by-chunk, then merged via a summary-of-summaries pass so the compaction request payload itself cannot blow past provider buffer limits. Set 0 to disable chunking.

settings.label: Write pre-compaction handoff artifact
settings.description: Before compaction, write a JSON handoff artifact under ~/.pi/agent/vstack/sessions/<session>/pi-qol/handoff/ containing the previous summary, last task state, and recently-referenced file/artifact paths. Always writes a stamped file and a latest.json pointer. Runs whenever the QOL bounded compaction handler runs — that is, on every budget-guard-triggered compaction (regardless of compaction.customEnabled), and on manual/idle compactions only when compaction.customEnabled is on. Write failures surface a QOL warning and a handoffArtifactError field in the compaction details.

settings.label: Transcript-risk warn budget (chars)
settings.description: Show a transcript-risk warning in /context when the serialized payload of the messages-to-send exceeds this many characters, even if the token count is still below the model context window. Set 0 to disable the warning.

settings.label: Hidden thinking label
settings.description: Label shown when thinking blocks are hidden. Defaults to a Nerd Font brain icon.

settings.label: Show thinking timer
settings.description: Show live elapsed time next to collapsed hidden-thinking labels and leave the final duration after thinking ends. Uses a defensive internal renderer patch and fails back to Pi's default label if Pi internals change.

settings.label: Working indicator mode
settings.description: Control Pi's built-in streaming spinner. The default animated spinner ticks every 80ms; in terminals where rendered content overflows the viewport (chat scrolled past the top, /tree open, etc.) each tick can trigger a pi-tui full-screen redraw and visible flash. Use 'static' for a single non-animating dot that keeps the working row visible without the 80ms timer.

author: vanillagreen
